reachability_fact: fed_admit.iii:179-181 (live, @export, called from corpus/240 line 145): `let qp : *u8 = qc_ptr` then `qp[40u64]..qp[43u64]` read with ZERO minimum-length guard. Any caller supplying a buffer shorter than 44 bytes causes OOB read before n_sigs is known. fed_seal.iii:85-86 has the identical pre-read OOB pattern (qp[40..43] before any length check). hs_verify_qc at hotstuff.iii:386 already does `if qc_len < 44u32 { return 0u8 }` but that guard is AFTER fed_admit/fed_seal have already indexed qp[40..43]. corpus/240:145 passes gen_root (32-byte malloc) as qc_ptr -- currently reaches E_POW before the QC path executes, which is why this survived corpus; the QC path itself is reachable for any call that passes the PoW+score gates.
maximal_design: Add `qc_len: u32` as the 5th parameter to `fed_admit_with_qc_proof` and to `fed_seal_anchor_with_qc`, giving both functions a real validated-length QC ABI. Inside each function, add three layered guards BEFORE indexing any QC byte: (1) `if qc_len < 44u32 { return E_QC/E_NULL }` -- minimum structural size before any read; (2) read n_sigs, then `if n_sigs > HS_MAX_PEERS (64u32) { return E_QC }` -- tighter than fed_seal's current 0xFFFF bound, matches the actual validator-set ceiling; (3) `if qc_len < 44u32 + n_sigs * 64u32 { return E_QC }` -- buffer adequacy for the full sig array. This is strictly better than the current code in every respect: it eliminates the pre-read OOB, the u32 wrap on n_sigs*64, and the too-loose 0xFFFF ceiling in fed_seal. Thread qc_len through both callers: corpus/240 extern decl + call site (pass 44u32 with a >=44-byte dummy buffer -- PoW fires first so E_POW result is preserved); corpus/386 extern decl + both call sites (pass 256u32 matching the existing Q_QC/Q_BADQC module globals). Add corpus 420 proving the minimum-length guard fires: call both functions with qc_len=10, a 10-byte zero buffer, and verify each returns the QC error code. That negative case is currently impossible to prove without the fix because the OOB read happens silently.

kat:
Corpus 420_fed_qc_len_guard.iii. Eight negative cases prove the fix fires on bad input:
- N1/N2/N3: fed_admit_with_qc_proof with qc_len 0/10/43 (all below the 44-byte minimum) must return FED_ADMIT_E_QC (-7), not crash or return anything else.
- N4/N5/N6: fed_seal_anchor_with_qc with qc_len 0/10/43 must return FED_SEAL_E_QC (-5).
- N7: 256-byte buffer with n_sigs=0xFFFFFFFF (would overflow 44+n_sigs*64 in u32); qc_len=256 passes minimum but n_sigs > 64 fires -> FED_ADMIT_E_QC (-7).
- N8: same overflow case for fed_seal -> FED_SEAL_E_QC (-5).
Positive case P1: qc_len=44, n_sigs=0 -- length guard passes; PoW fires -> FED_ADMIT_E_POW (-4). Proves the guard does not over-fire on structurally valid input.
Existing corpus 240 (exit 99) continues to prove E_POW on the PoW path with a 44-byte dummy buffer.
Existing corpus 386 (exit 99) continues to prove the full QC accept/reject round-trip for fed_seal.
